description:
`land` currently falls over when a rebase fails mechanically. Instead of aborting outright, the landing flow should preserve enough context to resubmit the worker through an LLM-assisted conflict resolution/retry path.

Scope:
- inspect the current land/rebase failure handling in `packages/pi-beadwork-extension/src/orchestrator.ts` and related worker/worktree helpers
- trigger the intended retry/resubmission flow when the failure is mechanical and recoverable
- make the retry path observable in logs/status so the operator can tell what happened
- add regression coverage for a mechanical rebase failure followed by a successful retry path

Notes:
- this overlaps with the other landing-flow tickets; prefer landing-lane work to happen in the same branch/worker sequence
- do not silently swallow unrecoverable failures; preserve actionable diagnostics

Definition of done:
- tests added/updated under `packages/pi-beadwork-extension/src/__tests__`
- `npm run lint -w @solvedbydev/pi-beadwork-extension`
- `npm run test -w @solvedbydev/pi-beadwork-extension`
- `npm run typecheck -w @solvedbydev/pi-beadwork-extension`
title: Auto-recover `land` from mechanical rebase failures with an LLM-assisted retry
